creator: rotkap@gmx.de
text:
Roman Bogorodskiy <novel@FreeBSD.org> wrote:

>   Heino Tiedemann wrote:
>
>> Roman Bogorodskiy <novel@FreeBSD.org> wrote:
>> 
>> >   Heino Tiedemann wrote:
>> >
>> >> Roman Bogorodskiy <novel@FreeBSD.org> wrote:
>> >> 
>> >> > Hi,
>> >> >
>> >> > Could you please describe how you installed easytag (from port, package,
>> >> > etc)? Also, it would be interesting to see output of 'ldd' for easytag.
>> >> 
>> >> Yes, I installed it from ports (I reinstalled it today with
>> >> portmaster).
>> >> 
>> >> 
>> >> 
>> >> ldd /usr/local/bin/easytag
>> >> /usr/local/bin/easytag:
>> >
>> > [stripped ldd output]
>> >
>> > Thanks for the ldd output, it looks alright at the first glance.
>> >
>> > Could you please re-compile easytag '-g' to produce a proper backtrace
>> > for easytag (with function names etc)?
>> >
>> > Sorry for inconvenience,
>> 
>> Hi,
>> 
>> I have to say sorry: How do I set '-g' to recompile?
>> 
>
> Please try to build with WITH_DEBUG flag, e.g. make -DWITH_DEBUG.

it is this:

(easytag:33102): Gtk-WARNING **: Invalid input string
EasyTAG version 2.1.8: Abnormal exit (PID: 33102).
Signal SIGSEGV (11) empfangen 
You have probably found a bug in EasyTAG. Please, file a bug report with a GDB backtrace ('gdb easytag core' then 'bt' and 'l') and information to reproduce it at: https://bugzilla.gnome.org/enter_bug.cgi?product=easytag
